channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Traffic has gotten way worse in these US cities, report finds
item.description: You're not imagining it. Traffic congestion worsened last year in just about every American city studied by navigation and GPS company TomTom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As the holiday honoring slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his teachings and work throughout the coming year, rather than just quoting him or celebrating for a day. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LIVE: Wildfire breaks out in Pacific Palisades area of Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters scrambled to corral flames churning along Los Angeles hillsides as winds gained strength Tuesday across Southern California, where forecasters warned of “life-threatening, destructive” gusts that could last for days while toppling trees, creating dangerous surf and br
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Forecasters warn of 'life-threatening, destructive' winds as fires break out in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters scrambled to corral flames churning along Los Angeles hillsides as winds gained strength Tuesday across Southern California, where forecasters warned of “life-threatening, destructive” gusts that could last for days while toppling trees, creating dangerous surf and br
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is back in Washington, where he remained an outsider
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: Less than two weeks before taking office, President-elect Donald Trump is taking some of his most audacious claims and promises of the transition period and amping them up to new levels.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Aamer Madhani And Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drone helps find Charlie the dog after he was missing for nearly a week
item.description: Charlie went out with the couple's other dog but when the two were called back in, he was nowhere to be found.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The plunging polar vortex brought subfreezing temperatures Tuesday to some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with X-style community notes
item.description: Bending to the political headwinds of the incoming Trump administration, Facebook and Instagram owner Meta is scrapping its third-party fact-checking program and replacing it with “community notes” written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social platform X.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Car abandoned on Colorado ski run; Driver blames GPS
item.description: A driver got a prime (but illegal) parking spot on the slopes Sunday night when their GPS led them right onto a ski run
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. arrives in Greenland after his father said the U.S. should own the Arctic territory
item.description: The eldest son of President-elect Donald Trump arrived in Greenland on Tuesday for a private visit that heightened speculation that the incoming U.S. administration could seek to take control of the mineral-rich Danish territory that's home to a large U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and VANESSA GERA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs fans' mysterious deaths yet to be resolved a year later
item.description: Clayton McGeeney, Ricky Johnson and David Harrington were found dead in Jordan Willis' Kansas City backyard on Jan. 9, 2024 — criminal charges have not been levied in their deaths.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's inauguration coincides with an unprecedented string of high-stakes security events in DC
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The last time a president was inaugurated, there was a global pandemic and a violent siege at the very spot at the U.S. Capitol where the ceremony takes place.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's praise of Carter in death after jeering him in life deepens a contradictory relationship
item.description: Living to 100 let Jimmy Carter fulfill his wish to vote for Democratic Vice President Kamala Harris against Republican Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Smart bird feeders gain popularity and spark interest in bird-watching
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Marin Plank truly had no interest in birds when she gave her husband a camera-equipped bird feeder for his birthday. But by Christmas, she had become so obsessed with birds that most of the gifts she received this year — books, stickers, notecards — were related.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Harris calls it a 'good day' for democracy as she oversees peaceful certification of Trump's victory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was no drama on Monday as Kamala Harris oversaw the ceremonial certification of her defeat to Donald Trump. The vice president kept her remarks perfunctory, and she stood with her hands clasped in front of her while the results from each state were read out loud.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, ABC 4

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's is the latest company to roll back diversity goals
item.description: Four years after launching a push for more diversity in its ranks, McDonald’s is ending some of its diversity practices, citing a U.S. Supreme Court decision that outlawed affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Monday for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a $148 million defamation judgment granted to two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gov. Kathy Hochul wants to triple New York's child tax credit
item.description: "The cost of living and raising a family is still too high," said Hochul. "We’re proposing a massive increase to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego bishop, Trump critic moved to Washington Diocese
item.description: The pope’s move put a vocal supporter of migrants at the top post in Washington just weeks before Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These winter driving 'tips' may be outdated and unnecessary, experts say
item.description: Driving in the winter is a different beast, especially when some of the most common advice for driving in the snow and cold isn't always right.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4
